entry_facets.label: Rule
entry_facets.summary: General Xianxia rule reference entries that do not need a narrower facet.

entry_facets.label: Attribute
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for the six Attributes used by Xianxia checks and character creation.

entry_facets.label: Effort
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Basic, Weapon, Guns/Explosive, Magic, and Ultimate Effort values.

entry_facets.label: Energy
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Jing, Qi, and Shen maximums, current pools, and spend hooks.

entry_facets.label: Yin/Yang
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Yin and Yang pools and their GM-clarified action support.

entry_facets.label: Dao
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Dao gain, cap, persistence, and spend reminders.

entry_facets.label: Realm
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Mortal, Immortal, and Divine realm state and action counts.

entry_facets.label: Honor Rank
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for Honor ranks and social interaction directionality.

entry_facets.label: Skill Rule
entry_facets.summary: Rules and references for trained freeform skills and their non-combat boundaries.

entry_facets.label: Equipment
entry_facets.summary: Entries for weapons, tools, and other Xianxia equipment that should stay outside DND-5E item assumptions.

entry_facets.label: Armor
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for armor as Defense-bearing gear while detailed armor quality tiers remain deferred.

entry_facets.label: Martial Art
entry_facets.summary: Parent entries for shared Xianxia Martial Arts, including seeded and GM-created arts.

entry_facets.label: Martial Art Rank
entry_facets.summary: Structured and embedded Martial Art Rank entries for Initiate, Novice, Apprentice, Master, and Legendary progress.

entry_facets.label: Technique
entry_facets.summary: Martial Art ability entries or rank-granted ability records tagged as Techniques.

entry_facets.label: Maneuver
entry_facets.summary: Martial Art ability entries or rank-granted ability records tagged as Maneuvers.

entry_facets.label: Stance
entry_facets.summary: Entries or ability records for one-active-Stance rules and Stance-granting Martial Art abilities.

entry_facets.label: Aura
entry_facets.summary: Entries or ability records for one-active-Aura rules and Aura-granting Martial Art abilities.

entry_facets.label: Generic Technique
entry_facets.summary: Standalone purchasable Generic Techniques and Maneuvers learned with Insight after character creation.

entry_facets.label: Basic Action
entry_facets.summary: Core Xianxia Basic Actions and basic Stances stored as reference-only Systems catalog entries.

entry_facets.label: Condition
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for condition concepts without automated condition timing, targeting, or combat-state effects in Milestone 1.

entry_facets.label: Status
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for status-effect concepts while the Xianxia status taxonomy and status functionality remain deferred.

entry_facets.label: Range Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Touch, Close, Near, Far, Space, and feet-based area dimensions.

entry_facets.label: Timing Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Turn, Round, Day, initiative order, and once-per-combat timing.

entry_facets.label: Critical Hit Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Critical Hit triggers and Ultimate Effort damage reminders.

entry_facets.label: Sneak Attack Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Sneak Attack as a special Critical Hit enabled by explicit circumstances.

entry_facets.label: Dying Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Dying state rules without implementing Dying Rounds tracking in Milestone 1.

entry_facets.label: Minion Tag
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for Minion-tagged NPC concepts without encounter seeding or combat automation.

entry_facets.label: Companion Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for puppet, phantom, animal, and similar companion derivation rules without richer companion automation.

entry_facets.label: GM Approval Rule
entry_facets.summary: Reference entries for explicit GM approval gates such as Karmic Constraints, Ascendant Arts, and Dao Immolating use.

martial_art_ranks.teacher_breakthrough_note: Requires learning under a Master. Teacher requirements are stored as notes for now rather than enforced advancement blockers.

apprentice: Evocation of Oaths' Remembrance Ritual Technique

novice: Bouncing Rain and Steel Thorns Iron Umbrella Stance

qi_fist_technique.text: You imbue your punches with an aura of raging Qi. Spend a point of Qi, for the rest of Combat your Unarmed Attacks do Weapon Effort Damage. Can harm incorporeal beings.

demon_s_rush_maneuver.text: Once per Turn, you may make a free additional Move action so long as it brings you closer to an enemy target.

armor_breaker_technique.text: When you Damage a Target you may spend a point of Jing to double the Damage.

rakshasa_s_flurry_maneuver.text: After successfully damaging a Target you may immediately Attack that Target again.

asura_s_wrath_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, You manifest 6 additional Spiritual Arms that help you out for the rest of the combat encounter. They add +5 to your Attack and Damage Rolls. When Damaged, you may choose to sacrifice an arm to negate all the Damage of that singular Attack. If a Target you are attacking has another target adjacent to them you may make a free attack against them as well, up to the number of arms you currently have.

directing_palm_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, any attacks made against you for the rest of the round are treated as HARD. Your Basic Unarmed Attacks always deal Double Stance Damage.

rolling_flow_maneuver.text: Once per Round, after being Attacked, you may choose to make a free Move action away from your assailant.

meridian_block_technique.text: When you Damage a Target you may instead choose to Block one of their Techniques or Maneuvers for the rest of the Round.

heavenly_foresight_maneuver.text: Once per Round you may choose to negate the results of a Combat you are in or Adjacent to.

buddha_s_palm_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen and Summon the Buddha's Palm from the Heavens to incapacitate all Targets within a 30x30 area for 3 Rounds. This does not cause damage however.

wudang_sword_wave.text: Spend a point of Qi. You may make a Ranged Attack of Qi towards a Target up to FAR Distance, Deals Magical Effort Damage

spirit_sight_maneuver.text: Spend a point of Shen, Your physical Attacks are now treated as Magical and can effect incorporeal beings for the rest of the Battle.

summoned_swords_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, Summon 3 additional Spirit Swords that float alongside you. When making an Attack you may consume one of the Swords to make an additional free Magical Attack or shoot the sword up to FAR distance for a Ranged Magical Attack.

taoist_way_maneuver.text: Once per Round you may reroll a failed roll once, you must take the new result.

eternal_trigram_sword_furnace_technique.text: Expend yourself of all Yin and Yang, anybody within NEAR Distance of you must do the same. For each point of Yin and Yang that was expended, each person within the Range of the Technique, except for the user, receives Damage equal to DOUBLE of the points spent.

drunken_sway_stance.text: Must be intoxicated first. When an attack fails to Damage you, you may immediately make a Counterattack. If you succeed you deal Double Stance Damage

foolish_strike_technique.text: After making an Attack, regardless if you succeed or fail, you may spend a point of Qi to Feint, Stunning your opponent for the rest of the Round.

tumbling_barrel_maneuver.text: Once per Round, when falling prone you may also choose to Move up to NEAR distance, or if prone you may choose to Move up to NEAR distance and stand up.

drunken_flow_misdirect_maneuver.text: When surrounded, Attacks that fail to Damage you instead automatically Damage the Enemies adjacent to you.

monkey_drinks_master_s_wine_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing and down a bottle of alcohol. For the rest of the Round, when your Attack or Counterattack Damages you may choose to steal a point of Jing or Qi from the Target. If you do so the Target must immediately make a HARD Attack on you.

preemptive_strike_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you may go first in initiative this Round.

coiling_snake_maneuver.text: Ranged Attacks that Target you are made HARD.

venomous_barb_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, your next successful attack with a Bo or Spear also inflicts Poison on the target. For the next 3 Rounds they will suffer Weapon Effort Damage at the beginning of their Turn.

hypnotic_hiss_maneuver.text: Spinning your Bo or Spear around at such a fast speed as to cause a hissing noise to be created, this noise causes all those within FAR Distance who fail a WIS Save to see multiples of you. Requiring a successful WIS save in order for any of their Attacks to be effective. Once saved this Maneuver will no longer have any effect on them for the rest of combat.

broken_fang_technique.text: Spend a point of Yang and Shen, when your next attack Damages a Target you may instead choose to seal one of their Techniques or Maneuvers for the rest of combat.

sanguine_sword_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing, your next successful attack deals additional Weapon Effort Damage.

bloodlusted_aura.text: Spend a point of Shen, for the rest of the Round opponents that enter combat with you are affected with Fear.

ravagers_dance_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing, you may make an immediate free attack on any enemy within Range.

bursting_body_maneuver.text: Automatically inflict 1d6 Damage on yourself, you may immediately make a free Move and Attack action. If Successful the Attack deals Double Damage and you heal Half of the Damage inflicted

demon_general_s_boiling_blood_technique.text: Inflict Energy Effort Damage upon yourself, all adjacent targets must make a CON save or be burned for Magical Effort for 3 turns. You may also choose to spend a further point of Jing to allow your Sanguine Sword Technique to now deal an additional Magical Effort Damage.

flaming_pinions_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi or Shen, Attack up to FAR Range 3 times on a Single Target, dealing Basic Effort.

burning_cleanse_aura.text: Spend a point of Shen, cleanse yourself of all Poisons or Burns

blazing_flight_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, Rush in a straight line up to FAR Distance long and NEAR Distance wide, All targets in the line must make a successful DEX or CON save or suffer Magical Effort Damage.

phoenix_tears.text: Once per round you automatically recover Energy Effort HP, you may further spend a point of Jing to recover an additional Energy Effort HP.

phoenix_descent_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, Manifest the Spiritual Phoenix to Burn the World Anew. The Phoenix Fights alongside you and cloaks you with searing flames that automatically cause Magical Effort Damage to anyone that enters combat against you. The Phoenix can perform a free Melee or Ranged attack a Round, using the User's Attributes +5. After 3 Rounds the Phoenix dissipates and leaves the user suffering Qi Deviation for the rest of combat. Qi Deviation causes the user to suffer Weapon Effort Damage every time they use a Martial Arts Technique.

dragon_s_flood_gate_technique.text: After a successful Attack, instead of dealing Damage you may recover a point of any Energy. Upon recovering this Energy you may immediately spend it and cause Magical Effort Damage on the Target.

leaping_carp_maneuver.text: Spend a point of Qi, you are able to ascend straight vertically with almost no limits. You no longer suffer Fall Damage.

destructive_tail_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, after performing an Attack you may also Attack all Targets within a NEAR Distance Cone of you, dealing Magical Effort Damage.

dragon_s_pearl.text: You can condense your Qi into a small carriable Orb that can be used to recover Magical Effort HP, Recover 1 Point Energy, or as a small explosive Artifact with NEAR Distance Ultimate Effort Damage +5 AoE burst.

dragon_s_ascent_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you manifest the Spiritual Dragon to overtake the heavens. The Dragon's form envelops you and raises you into the air, taking flight for the duration of this Technique. Gain +5 to all combat checks and access to the Dragon's Breath, a conal frontal AoE Attacking Technique that requires a successful DEX or CON save. A failed Save causes Magical Effort Damage, and inflicts a Burn for Weapon Effort Damage over the next 3 Rounds. Dragon's Breath can only be used once per Round. After 3 Rounds the Dragon Dissipates and leaves the user suffering Qi Deviation for the rest of combat. Qi Deviation causes the user to suffer Weapon Effort Damage every time they use a Martial Arts Technique.

rooted_mountain_stance.text: Spend a point of Jing, gain +10 Temporary HP and Stance. Become impervious to knockdowns and forced movements.

mammoth_s_bullrush_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing, your next successful Attack knocks the Target prone.

trampling_maneuver.text: If you Move through an enemy occupied space they must make a successful DEX or CON save or take Weapon Effort Damage. Can destroy small boulders or thin walls by running through them.

iron_hide_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing or a point of Qi, gain +10 Defense for the rest of the Round. Until the end of the Round add your Defense Bonus to Damage Dealt.

trembling_heavens_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you strike the ground and all airborne creatures in combat fall to the earth. They are unable to fly for the next 3 Rounds.

whipping_branch_stance.text: Spend a point of Qi, Gain +3 Defense and Recover Magic Effort Stance. If an Attack hits you, lose this Defense bonus.

snap_kick_technique.text: If an Attack misses you, you may spend a point of Jing to make an EASY Counterattack.

entangled_roots_maneuver.text: Those who are engaged in Melee Combat with you must make a HARD DEX roll against you in order to disengage. You may spend a point of Shen to have them Automatically fail this roll.

raining_parry_technique.text: When an Attack misses you, you may spend a point of Qi to gain an additional +3 Defense until the end of the Round. May spend a point of Shen to completely negate a single Physical Attack.

wind_s_whispering_smoke_maneuver.text: Spend a point of Shen and Qi, gain +10 Defense for the rest of Combat. When you would take damage you may instead choose to lower your Defense by -2. Physical Attacks do half Damage to you. You can not use this Maneuver again until your Defense bonus is used up.

sonic_boom_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi and play your instrument, fire off a sound based FAR Ranged Attack that deals Energy Effort Damage.

melodic_malady_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi and play your instrument, all enemies within NEAR Distance of you must make a successful CON save or all of their checks will be made HARD for the rest of the Round. All Allies within the same Distance automatically have their next roll made EASY.

metronome_maneuver.text: Once per Round, when any character in your line of sight Moves you may choose to follow them an equal distance.

sound_barrier_burst_technique.text: When you or an ally up to FAR Distance of you take Damage you may spend a point of Qi and play your instrument and reduce it by Energy Effort. You may also use this Offensively, spending a point of Qi and play your instrument, dealing automatic Energy Effort Damage to all Characters in NEAR Distance of you. They must also make a CON or DEX save or be pushed back up to NEAR Distance.

heaven_s_defying_hymn_technique.text: Once per character per combat, spend a point of Qi and Shen and play your instrument, you may resurrect a fallen character back to full HP and Stance.

puppeteering_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you imbue a Puppet with faux life allowing it to act semi-autonomously. It is completely under your control and does Weapon Effort in Damage. It uses Stats equal to half of yours. May only control one at a time.

substitution_maneuver.text: When Attacked you may swap places with your Puppet. Can not be done if the Puppet is destroyed.

hidden_inventory_technique.text: When you Attack or are Attacked you may spend a point of Jing or Qi, adding additional Weapon Effort Damage or increasing your Defense by Weapon Effort.

master_of_puppets_technique.text: You are now able to create and control any number of Puppets. When creating a Puppet you may spend a further point of Shen to have that Puppet's Stats be equal to yours.

spirit_strings_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen and perform a successful unarmed melee Attack on a Target with either you or your Puppet. The Target must then make a successful CHA save or become controlled by you for the next 3 Rounds. If you drop to 0 HP you may spend a point of Shen and Jing to Puppet yourself. You are considered unconscious and dying as you do this making all INT, WIS, and CHA checks HARD. If you take any further Damage in this state your Dying Rounds are decreased by -1.

tiger_s_claw_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing, your next successful Attack deals double Stance Damage. If you Break the Target’s Stance, immediately Deal Basic Effort Damage to them.

pouncing_follow_up_maneuver.text: After a successful Tiger Style Technique, if your opponent's Stance is less than half, you may make another Free Attack against the Target.

pinning_sweep_technique.text: After a successful Attack, spend a point of Qi, your opponent must make a successful DEX save or be knocked prone. They can not arise again until they make a successful DEX save.

tiger_s_fang_technique.text: After a successful Tiger's Claw you may spend a further point of Shen to follow up with a Tiger's Fang, this Attack is made EASY and deals direct HP Damage.

tiger_s_vessel.text: Once per combat spend a point of Shen and allow the spirit of the Great White Tiger to possess you. You enter into a Berserk State and Attack the nearest Target, your Attacks gain +5 To Hit and +5 to Damage. All your Attacks while in this State deal direct HP Damage. After 3 Rounds the possession ends and leaves the user suffering Qi Deviation for the rest of combat. Qi Deviation causes the user to suffer Weapon Effort Damage every time they use a Martial Arts Technique.

wrestler_s_stance.text: Grapples are made EASY for you. Once the Target is grappled you may spend a point of Jing to immediately deal Basic Effort Damage to the Target.

takedown_and_throw_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, you may move in a straight line up to NEAR Distance to grapple a Target, if successful you knock them prone and deal additional Basic Effort Damage.

tumble_and_roll_maneuver.text: If you are grappling a Target and are Attacked, or if they attempt to break out of the grapple, you may spend a point of Jing and roll up to NEAR Distance with the Target in tow. At the end of the Movement you must toss the Target up to NEAR Distance away from you, dealing an additional Basic Effort Damage.

sprawling_lock_technique.text: After a successful grapple, anytime your Target tries to break the grapple you may spend a point of Jing causing their attempt to automatically fail and dealing additional Basic Effort Damage.

meridian_stranglehold.text: When a Target is in a grapple you may spend 2 points of Qi to block the flow of their Meridians. While they are grappled by you in this state they can not use any of their Martial Arts Techniques or Maneuvers.

ice_manipulation_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, you can control ice and even use it as a dangerous icicle projectile (FAR Range) or form sharp weaponry (Melee). These deal Magical Effort Damage.

frozen_body_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you can freeze your body and form a thin barrier of crystalline ice to protect you from Attack. Reduce received Damage by Half for the rest of the Round.

blizzard_s_haze_maneuver.text: Blinding chilled winds swirl around you in battle making your movements hard to discern. Ranged Attacks made against you are always HARD and Movement is slowed within a NEAR Range of you.

chilling_drain_touch_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, your next successful Melee Attack lowers your Target's maximum HP by half of the Damage dealt for the rest of combat.

death_s_touch_deep_freeze_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, Jing, and Shen, Once per combat you freeze your hand a deep black and cold. The next person you successfully grapple has their lifeforce rapidly drained from them, sending them instantly into a dying state.

misfortune_warding_talisman_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you form a sticky Talisman that gives one of 3 Effects: +3 Attack/Damage, +3 Defense, or +3 to Skill Checks.

sealing_talisman_technique.text: Spend a point of Shen, you form a sticky Talisman, and make a Melee Attack. On a success your Target can not spend a point of Energy or Yin/Yang for 1 Round.

delayed_explosion_effect.text: After applying a Talisman you may additionally spend a point of Shen or Qi, after 3 Rounds the Talisman will explode either Healing or Damaging the Target by Ultimate Effort.

spirit_walk_maneuver.text: Spend a point of Shen, you become incorporeal and enter into the Spirit realm. This allows you to directly fight these kinds of foes, all of your Attacks are considered Magical Effort Damage while in this state.

10_000_year_demon_furnace_sealing_techni.text: Spend a point of Shen, a point of Yin, and a point of Yang. For the rest of combat your Target must make either a successful WIS or CON save or suffer from a Chain of Sealing. Each Chain of Sealing does Ultimate Effort Damage and gives an accumulative -2 to their checks. Once the Target gets 5 Chains of Sealing they are immediately restricted and dragged into the deepest layer of Naraka. While performing this Technique the user is incapable of doing anything else.

wavering_strike_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, when you successfully Attack gain +1 Dao. Dao gained this way is automatically spent at the end of the Round if not used, regaining +5 Stance for each point left over.

shaper_s_ripple_maneuver.text: When you spend a point of Dao allies within NEAR Distance of you gain +1 Dao for this Round.

formless_stance.text: If not currently in a Stance, and is not Stance Broken, automatically enter this Stance when gaining Dao. When in this Stance choose to gain either an Attack Bonus or Defense bonus equal to 3+Dao, you may change this bonus at the end of the Round. When Attacking in this Stance you may Spend a point of Dao to immediately switch to a different Stance at the end of the Action.

initiative_of_heavenly_dao_maneuver.text: You may spend a point of Dao to swap initiative order with any other Combatant in the battle, even before the beginning of theirs/your Turn. This can only be done once per Round. Swapping to a lower Initiative gives you a Defense bonus equal to 10+The difference in Initiative. Swapping to a higher initiative gives you an Attack bonus equal to 10+The difference.

foresight_of_the_true_dao.text: Note: the True Dao is unknowable. Once a day outside of Combat you can spend a point of Dao and Shen, roll 3d20’s and keep the highest result in Reserve. When in Combat you may choose to forgo rolling and use this Reserved Result instead. Once per Round you may use a point of Dao as a Hero Point, doing so gives the user 1 point of Negative Karma. For each point of Negative Karma the Damage received is increased by +10.

iaido_draw_technique.text: Your weapon must be sheathed. Spend a point of Qi, you immediately draw your weapon and make a Melee Attack dealing Weapon Effort+Ultimate. This Attack is made EASY.

bushido_stance.text: Spend a point of Jing and enter into the Bushido Stance. Your Movement is halved while in this Stance. Gain +3 to Attack and Damage, when your Stance is dropped to 0 you may decide to take HP Damage instead and leave Stance at 1.

stampeding_barricade_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, your next Successful Attack deals bonus Damage equal to your current Stance. Regain HP equal to half of the Damage dealt.

tsubame_gaeshi_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi and Jing, your next Attack is made EASY, your Target must make a Successful HARD DEX check, if they fail they immediately suffer a second automatically Successful Attack from you that deals additional Ultimate Damage.

warrior_s_death.text: Spend a point of Qi, Jing, and Shen. Drop your HP and Stance to 1, for the next 3 Rounds no amount of Damage can drop you to 0 nor can any Attacks, Magic, or Status Effects knock you unconscious or to Dying. Your Attacks automatically Hit and always count as Criticals. At the end of the 3 Rounds, if the Combat encounter is still going, you immediately die standing.

scholar_s_memorabilia_technique.text: Spend a Point of Shen to summon a Phantom of History, this Phantom is one time use and performs a specific Action related to the Historical individual that it is based off. Use your Stats +3.

vividness_recall.text: Spend a Point of Shen and a Turn, OR prepare it beforehand, summon a Phantom of History. This Phantom stays on the field for 3 Rounds and helps assist in Action related to its historical life. Use your Stats+3. Can be talked to.

evocation_of_oaths_remembrance_ritual_te.text: Spend a point of Shen and Jing to give the Phantom an embodied form and spend a period of time equal to the difficulty of the task, then burn a stick of incense. Phantom uses your Stats+5, these Embodied Phantoms are autonomous however they are bound to fulfilling their mission above all else. The Embodied Phantom continues to exist so long as the incense burns, the mission is not complete, and it is not destroyed. Embodied can be hurt by physical attacks.

blotting_strike_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi or Shen, Attack up to FAR Range on a Single Target, dealing Magical Effort Damage. On a Hit, all enemy Targets up to 3 squares away take Basic Effort Damage.

inkblind_veil_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, choose a Target at FAR range. The Target either gains a bonus to Defense or a penalty to Attack equal to your Basic Effort bonus until your next turn. If the target is not willing, must make an attack roll for this to take effect.

the_animal_bond.text: You have an implicit link to a mundane animal of your choosing. This animal can be communicated with and will follow orders up to an extent of its trust. If this animal dies you may establish a new Bond with another mundane animal. The Animal uses your Stats +3 in things related to its strengths: e.g. Bear will have +3 Strength, Rat +3 Dexterity, etc.

the_forging_bond_technique.text: Upon gaining this Technique you may now spend time to forge a new bond with another mundane animal, this does not break your current bond. In battle you may spend a point of Shen to imbue one bonded animal with Fighting Spirit? allowing them to act as a participant in combat. They will use your Stats +3 in Attributes related to its strengths and deal your Magical Effort in Damage. They act immediately after your Turn.

follow_the_leader_maneuver.text: After an Attack, or if flanking an enemy target with your animal companion, you can either choose to spend a point of Qi OR Jing. If you spend a point of Qi, make the animal companion’s next Attack EASY and increase Temp Stance by +3 (Up to a max of half of the user’s Stance). If you spend a point of Jing, the animal companion’s next Attack deals an additional accumulative +2 Damage (Max of +10) and increase Temp HP by +3 (Up to a max of half of the user’s HP

follow_the_leader_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, You play your instrument to a specified group of mundane animals, and they must obey you for the next 10 minutes. They are only able to perform simple tasks, gain +3 to tasks related to their capability.

primordial_harmony_maneuver.text: At the end of your Turn, if you used a Instrument Martial Arts Technique, you may spend a point of Shen and buff an Animal Companion by +3 Attack, Defense, and Damage.

flashing_blade_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, Your weapon must be sheathed/hidden, you immediately draw your weapon and make an Attack using its Range dealing Weapon Effort+Ultimate. This can only be done on the first Round of Combat OR if your Target is focusing on another opponent. This Attack is made EASY

bouncing_rain_and_steel_thorns_iron_umbr.text: Spend a point of Qi and you enter into this Stance. While this Stance is Active you gain +3 Defense and take half Damage. Attacks made to Move or Knock you Prone are made HARD. If you have an Active Aura you may also spend a point of Jing to Reflect up to 5 Damage you receive per Attack while this Stance is Active.

toxic_blood_coat_technique.text: Spend a point of Jing, and deal Weapon Effort Damage to self [HP damage], you coat your weapons with your blood and imbue them with strength. Your Attacks with these weapons now deal additional Magic Damage and Targets that take damage must make a CON save or be poisoned for Basic Effort for 3 turns.

luocha_s_vengeful_aura.text: When you lose HP you may spend a point of Shen to activate this Aura. Everyone within the Aura must make a successful CHA check or be inflicted with FEAR for the rest of the Round. 5ft Aura, ends at the start of your Turn UNLESS a point of HP is spent.

apparition_s_ghastly_beauty_aura.text: Spend a point of Shen to activate, while the Aura is active enemies that catch sight of you must make a successful CHA check to resist being drawn in closer to you. If within NEAR distance while this Aura is active they must make a CHA check every time they perform an Attack. On a failure they become distracted and their Attack is made HARD, if Targeting you they hold back at the last moment causing the Attack to fail. While distracted, Attacks against the Target are made EASY and count as Sneak Attacks.

seeded_lilac_s_curse_technique.text: Requires Apparition's Ghastly Beauty Aura to be active. When an enemy successfully Attacks you while your Aura is active you may spend a point of Shen to inflict a point of Curse upon the Target, up to a maximum of 3. Each point of Curse deals Magical Effort Damage at the start of the Target's Turn, lowering Curse by 1 at the end of their Turn.

explosive_encore_technique.text: When you kill a Target with an Instrument Based Martial Arts Technique you may spend a point of Shen to immediately cause a Magical AoE Explosion up to NEAR Distance.

eagle_eyed_insight_maneuver.text: At the start of a combat where you are not surprised, you and allies gain a bonus to Initiative equal to your bonus to Magical Effort.) Gain the Flow of Battle Maneuver. (At the end of the round, you or an ally may choose to increase or decrease their Initiative value by an amount equal to your basic effort.

flow_of_battle_maneuver.text: At the end of the round, you or an ally may choose to increase or decrease their Initiative value by an amount equal to your basic effort.

tactician_s_heavenly_gaze_stance.text: While in this stance, you gain a -3 penalty to Attack rolls and to Defense. Allies within NEAR distance gain a +3 bonus to Attack rolls. At the end of your turn, you and all allies within NEAR distance gain Temporary Stance equal to Basic effort and may Move equal to half their movement. When doing so, you may spend a Dao to increase the Temporary Stance gained by +5.

the_broken_tiger_s_vessel.text: You have enhanced senses, making ALL Perception checks EASY. At night gain Basic Regeneration, at the start of your Turn Regain 2 HP. Once per Day you may spend a point of Jing and Shen to transform into your Cursed Tiger Form. All Combat related Checks are made EASY and your Attacks deal an additional +3 Damage, however you lose human sense and reasoning and ALWAYS attack the nearest Target possible.

the_tiger_s_domain.text: Can now speak to mundane Felines whether wild or domesticated, your HP Regain is enhanced allowing you to Regain 2 HP during the day and 4 at Night, once per Turn you may spend a point of Jing to add +1d6 to Healing. At night you may now spend a point of Shen to initiate a 10ft Aura around you. Those that enter your Aura must make a successful CHA check before each Attack or they hold back from intimidation.

symbiotic_reverse_possession_technique.text: Spend 2 points of Jing, to enter into your Primal Beast Form. Gain +3 to all Physical Stats and to sense based Actions, your physical attacks deal additional Ultimate Damage, and your Movement is doubled. This form lasts for 1d4 Rounds, you may extend this form by spending an extra point of Shen or Jing at the end of its duration. This form can only be activated once per combat. This form is Pseudo-Spiritual, as such you take half Damage from physical attacks.

gathering_slipstream_stance.text: Spend a point Qi, enter into the Gathering Slipstream Stance. While in this Stance you imbue your weapons with Wind Elemental energy turning them into DEX weapons. If you begin a Round while in this Stance gain +1 Slipstream, increasing Attack bonus, Damage, and Defense by an equal amount of Slipstream up to a maximum of +5. At +5 Slipstream gain +1 Action.

bursting_gust_maneuver.text: Must be in Gathering Slipstream Stance, when Attacked you may spend a point of Slipstream and either gain +3 Defense OR 1 Free Movement Action after the Attack’s completion.

sonic_break_technique.text: Must be in Gathering Slipstream Stance. At +5 Slipstream you may expend all points to perform the Sonic Break Technique. This Melee Attack is made EASY, deals additional Ultimate Damage, and you may push the Target up to NEAR Distance in any direction of your choosing. If the Attack is unsuccessful you fail to do Damage but you may still move the Target in a chosen Direction.

skybound_tether_technique.text: Spend a point of Qi, make an Attack on a Target, place, or thing. You bind yourself to that Target until the tether is broken, while tethered you may make one free Action to pull yourself towards the Target or pull the Target towards you, putting you adjacent to the Target. If pulled, the Target must make a successful STR check to resist.

fowler_s_snare_technique.text: After a successful Attack you may spend a point of Jing and choose 1 of 3 Effects:  1. Mark the Target, the Marked Target takes +3 additional Damage from you and your Allies. Attacks Crit against this Target on a natural roll of 18+ 2. Kick up Dust, the Target becomes the epicenter of a Dust Cloud that covers an area around them up to NEAR Distance. All characters within the Dust Cloud have their Attacks made HARD. 3 Rounds. 3. Strip Up, the Target is caught off balance and they must make a successful DEX check. On a success their Movement is halved next Turn, on a failure they are knocked Prone.

cultivation.text: Increase an Energy by +1 (Can be taken multiple times.)

meditation.text: Increase Yin or Yang by +1 (Can be taken multiple times.)

conditioning.text: +10 HP OR +2 Effort Points (Can be taken multiple times. Max HP of 50.)

training.text: +10 Stance OR +2 Attribute Points (Can be taken multiple times. (Max Stance of 50.)

qi_blast.text: Spend a point of Qi, fire a Ranged Qi Attack up to NEAR Distance (2 Qi for FAR) that deals Magical Effort Damage.

prerequisites.label: Target's highest Martial Art or Realm is lower, or the target is less Honorable/Reputable

scolding_backhand.text: Deal additional Basic Effort Damage and make their next roll HARD if the Target's highest Martial Art or Realm is lower than yours, or if they are less Honorable/Reputable than you. Can only be done once a Combat.

prerequisites.label: High place or exceedingly windy area

wind_glide.text: You can naturally glide upon the winds up to FAR Distance (need to leap off a high place or the area must be exceedingly windy)

great_leap.text: Spend a point of Jing and leap up to Double FAR Distance

meteor_walk.text: Requires Immortal Status. Spend a point of Shen to temporarily become energy and enter into the flow of Ley Lines. Being able to travel long distances along these pre-established paths within a much shorter timeframe.

flight.text: Requires Immortal Status. Spend a point of Qi to enter into Flight, Move at standard rate during Combat. Move at a rate of 6 Distance during Travel.

duelist.text: Gain +2 Attack and +3 Damage while in a Duel. If your opponent attempts to Disengage from the Duel you may spend a point of Jing or Qi to force the Target to make a DEX/STR Check to successfully Disengage.

flash_step.text: Spend a point of Jing, your next Movement is Doubled

speed_blitz.text: At the beginning of Combat you may immediately Move to an adjacent space/CLOSE Combat with any enemy within FAR Distance

graceful_balance.text: You can stand upon even the most narrow or unstable surfaces without penalty

kip_up.text: No longer needs to spend an Action to get up from Prone.

water_run.text: You can Move across water as though it were ground

yin_healing.text: Spend a point of Yin and Heal Magical Effort on Target or Self

yin_cleansing.text: Spend a point of Yin and cleanse any DoT or Status Effect on a Target you are Touching or Self (Except Qi Deviation)

yin_regeneration.text: Spend a point of Yin Heal Magical Effort for the next 3 Rounds.

yin_fortification.text: Spend a point of Yin and gain an additional +3 Defense for the next 3 Rounds

yang_enhancement.text: Spend a point of Yang and gain an additional +3 to your Attack rolls for the next 3 Rounds

yang_sundering.text: When you spend a point of Yang, whether for an Attack or Damage bonus, add an additional +2d6 to your next successful Attack's Damage. Your Target can not recover Stance for 1 Round.

two_finger_disarmament.text: If Attacked by a Weapon, roll an Unarmed Counterattack if yours is Higher you disarm your assailant. If you are of a Higher Realm you do this automatically.

throat_crush.text: Spend an Action and aim for the throat and force your Target to make a HARD CON check. If they fail they are unable to speak or perform incantations for 2 Rounds.

knuckle_splitter.text: Spend an Action and strike your Target’s fingers with painful precision. Target must make a HARD DEX Check, if they fail they are unable to perform Mudras for 2 Rounds.

cyclone_barrage.text: You may choose to make an Area of Effect Attack against all Targets within NEAR Distance of you. However this Attack can only deal Basic (If Unarmed) or Weapon Effort Damage (If Armed).

disorientating_palm_strike.text: Spend a point of Qi, your next Successful Unarmed Attack deals additional Basic Effort Stance Damage. Your Target must make a Successful CON check or lose 1 Action next Turn.

prerequisites.label: First time your Attack drops a Target's Stance to 0

pressing_stance_break.text: The first time your Attack drops a Target’s Stance to 0 deal Double Damage. For 1 Round they can’t recover Stance.

piercing_blow.text: Spend a point of Qi, Half of the Damage of your next Successful Unarmed Attack deals direct HP Damage

cushioning_sway.text: Spend a point of Jing, the next Successful physical Attack made against you deals 10 less Damage. Must Move 5ft away from the Attacker at the end of the Action.

enhanced_recuperation.text: When you use the Recoup Action you recover 10+CON Stance.

enhanced_recollection.text: When you use the Recollect Action you recover 1d3 Points of Energy

enhanced_flowing_dao.text: Gain an additional +1 Action or Regain 10 HP/Stance

timing_notes: Spend an Action and 1 Energy of any kind to recover Stance.
Spend the Turn meditating to recover 1 point of any Energy.

range_notes: Duel starts after a Melee Attack; disengaging moves one Space away from the former opponent.
timing_notes: Ranged Attacks are HARD while the Duel remains active.
On success, the target must spend at least one Action moving toward or attacking you next Turn.

range_notes: Three Spaces equals 15 feet under the Xianxia Space definition.

timing_notes: Spend 1 Dao once per Turn to gain +1 Action or regain HP/Stance.
On a failed CON check, the target cannot vocalize or use incantations for 1 Round.
On a failed DEX check, the target cannot use Mudras for 1 Round.
The Defense and damage-reduction modifiers last while the Stance is active.
The Attack, Defense, and damage modifiers last while the Stance is active.
The Stance prevents movement while active; a missed opposing Attack can trigger an immediate Counter-Attack.

entries.title: Checks and Difficulty
entries.summary: Xianxia checks roll 1d20 plus an Attribute, Realm modifier, and situational modifiers against a final DC.

sections.title: Check Formula
paragraphs: A normal check is 1d20 + Attribute + Realm modifier + situational modifiers. Add +1d6 for each point of appropriate Energy, Yin, or Yang spent. A result that equals or exceeds the final DC succeeds.

entries.title: EASY, Normal, and HARD
entries.summary: EASY, Normal, and HARD are final DC states, not unbounded stacking bonuses.

sections.title: DC States
bullets: EASY lowers the final DC by 3.
Normal leaves the final DC unchanged.
HARD raises the final DC by 3.
EASY and HARD influences shift the final state against each other instead of stacking into larger bonuses or penalties.

entries.title: Attributes
entries.summary: Xianxia uses the six standard Attributes for checks and character creation budgets.

sections.title: Attribute Set
bullets: The six Attributes are Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma.
Starting characters spend 6 creation points across Attributes.
No Attribute can exceed 3 at character creation unless the GM explicitly overrides the budget.

entries.title: Efforts and Damage
entries.summary: Efforts are separate from Attributes and add their score to the matching effort die for damage.

sections.title: Effort Families
bullets: Basic Effort uses 1d4 + Basic.
Weapon Effort uses 1d6 + Weapon.
Guns/Explosive Effort uses 1d8 + Guns/Explosive.
Magic Effort uses 1d10 + Magic.
Ultimate Effort uses 1d12 + Ultimate.

sections.title: Creation Budget
bullets: Starting characters spend 5 creation points across Efforts.
No Effort can exceed 3 at character creation unless the GM explicitly overrides the budget.

entries.title: Energy: Jing, Qi, and Shen
entries.summary: Jing, Qi, and Shen are spendable Energies with separate maximums and action themes.

sections.title: Energy Uses
bullets: Jing supports physical actions.
Qi supports mystical or skill-based actions.
Shen supports spiritual or magical actions.
A character may spend an appropriate Energy point for +1d6 on a check.
Starting characters spend 3 creation points across Jing, Qi, and Shen.

entries.title: Yin and Yang
entries.summary: Yin and Yang are separate spendable pools that start at 1 each.

sections.title: GM-Ruled Action Support
bullets: Yin supports offensive or physical actions.
Yang supports defensive or magical actions.
A character may spend an appropriate Yin or Yang point for +1d6 on a check.
Yin and Yang recover with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Dao
entries.summary: Dao is a capped narrative and combat resource that persists until spent.

sections.title: Dao Rules
bullets: Dao is capped at 3.
Dao starts at 0 unless the GM explicitly grants accumulated Dao.
Dao may be gained through Energy spend, roleplay, or certain Martial Arts.
Dao does not reset at session start or after rest.
Dao is held until spent on difficulty reduction or later Dao actions.

entries.title: Hit Points
entries.summary: Hit Points are the character's injury track and start with a maximum of 10.

sections.title: HP Baseline
bullets: Starting HP maximum is 10.
Current HP is clamped to the current HP maximum.
Dropping to 0 HP puts the character in the Dying state.
HP recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Stance
entries.summary: Stance is a second durability track that starts with a maximum of 10.

sections.title: Stance Baseline
bullets: Starting Stance maximum is 10.
Current Stance is clamped to the current Stance maximum.
When current Stance reaches 0, the character's Stance breaks.
Stance recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.

entries.title: Defense
entries.summary: Defense is derived from base 10, armor bonus, and Constitution.

sections.title: Formula
paragraphs: Defense equals 10 + armor bonus + Constitution. Milestone 1 uses a manual armor bonus until detailed armor quality and stat tiers are defined.

entries.title: Insight
entries.summary: Insight is the advancement currency for Martial Arts, cultivation, training, meditation, conditioning, and Generic Techniques.

sections.title: Insight Baseline
bullets: Insight starts at 0.
Generic Techniques cannot be learned at character creation because they require spending Insight unless the GM grants an exception.
Martial Art teacher requirements are notes for now rather than enforced advancement blockers.

entries.title: Realm and Actions
entries.summary: Realm defines major character tier, action count, and ascension rebuild rules.

sections.title: Realm Actions
bullets: Mortal characters have 2 total Actions.
Immortal characters have 3 total Actions.
Divine characters have 4 total Actions.

sections.title: Ascension Reference
bullets: Immortal ascension requires 1 year of seclusion, rebuilds Attributes and Efforts with 15 points, and has a starting max of 6 in any one Stat.
Divine ascension requires 100 years of seclusion, rebuilds Attributes and Efforts with 25 points, and has a starting max of 12 in any one Stat.
Realm ascension resets only Attributes and Efforts; Energies, Yin/Yang, HP, Stance, Insight, Martial Arts, Generic Techniques, and other character data remain unchanged.

entries.title: Honor
entries.summary: Honor is a directional social modifier tied to orthodox, demonic, and criminal contexts.

sections.title: Honor Interactions
bullets: Honorable is neutral and is the default starting Honor.
Venerable and Majestic grant +3 and +5 with orthodox sects and individuals.
Disgraced and Demonic grant +3 and +5 with demonic or criminal backgrounds.
When dealing with the opposite Honor alignment, the same value applies as a penalty.

entries.title: Reputation
entries.summary: Reputation starts at Unknown and is a narrative roleplay convenience rather than a hard mechanic.

sections.title: Reputation Baseline
paragraphs: Reputation should record how a character is known in the world, but Milestone 1 does not treat it as an automated check modifier.

entries.title: Skills
entries.summary: Skills are freeform trained non-combat specialties that can make relevant checks EASY.

sections.title: Skill Guardrails
bullets: Starting characters choose exactly 3 trained skills.
A trained skill can make a relevant check EASY.
Skills cannot be used in active battle to affect Attacks or Damage.
Skills can affect surroundings or pre-battle preparation when the GM agrees.

entries.title: Basic Actions
entries.summary: Basic Actions are core Xianxia action references; individual automation remains deferred.

sections.title: Known Basic Actions
paragraphs: The core Basic Action reference covers Recoup, Recollect, Duel, Taunt, Wide Dispatch, Flowing Dao, Throat Jab, Knuckle Strike, Defend, All-Out Offense, and Parry.

entries.title: Cultivation Time
entries.summary: Cultivation time records advancement downtime and GM-reviewed growth rather than a combat automation loop.

sections.title: Downtime Reference
bullets: Gathering Insight is a downtime gain path.
Cultivation, Meditation, Conditioning, Training, Martial Art rank advancement, and Generic Technique learning spend Insight later in advancement workflows.
Realm ascension seclusion uses the Realm timing rules: 1 year for Immortal and 100 years for Divine.
Milestone 1 does not add calendar automation for cultivation time.

entries.title: One-Day Rest
entries.summary: One day of rest recovers the normal Xianxia pools except Dao unless another effect blocks recovery.

sections.title: Recovery
bullets: Outside battle, HP recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Outside battle, Stance recovers with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Energy, Yin, and Yang recover with one day of rest unless another effect prevents recovery.
Dao does not reset after rest and remains held until spent.

entries.title: Ranges and Distance
entries.summary: Xianxia range labels map to feet, adjacency, and 5-foot spaces.

sections.title: Range Labels
bullets: Touch requires physical contact through a Melee Attack.
Close means within 5 feet, adjacent, or dueling.
Near means up to 30 feet.
Far means up to 60 feet.
One Space is a 5-foot square.
Area dimensions such as 30x30 are measured in feet.

entries.title: Timing and Initiative
entries.summary: Timing terms define turns, rounds, days, initiative order, and once-per-combat limits.

sections.title: Timing Terms
bullets: A Turn is one character's time for Actions in combat.
A Round is the full pass through every participant's Turn in initiative order.
Initiative is set at the beginning of combat with a Dex Check, runs highest to lowest, and is not rerolled each Round.
A Day is 24 hours.
Once-per-combat means once per combat encounter.

entries.title: Stance Activation Rules
entries.summary: A character can have only one Stance active at a time.

sections.title: Active Stance
bullets: Entering a Stance costs an Action plus any Stance-specific costs.
A Stance ends when the character's Stance breaks.
A Stance ends when the character switches Stances.
A Stance ends when the character can no longer maintain the Stance's requirements.

entries.title: Aura Activation Rules
entries.summary: A character can have only one Aura active at a time.

sections.title: Active Aura
paragraphs: Auras are assumed to remain active once activated unless the Aura says otherwise or the GM overrules it.

entries.title: Dying and Unconsciousness
entries.summary: Dropping to 0 HP puts a character in the Dying state; Dying Rounds tracking remains deferred automation.

sections.title: Dying Reference
bullets: When a character drops to 0 HP, the player rolls 1d6; the result is the number of Dying Rounds available.
During those Dying Rounds, the character must roll a natural 20 on a raw d20, be healed by an ally, receive item healing, or have the battle end to stop dying.
A dying character is unconscious, cannot take Actions, cannot interact or speak, and is unaware of their surroundings.
Milestone 1 stores this as reference text and does not implement Dying Rounds tracking.

entries.title: Critical Hits
entries.summary: Critical Hits automatically hit and add Ultimate Effort damage.

sections.title: Critical Hit Reference
paragraphs: Critical Hits occur on a natural 20, or on a lower natural roll when modified by a Martial Art or Technique. Critical Hits automatically hit and deal additional +Ultimate Effort damage.

entries.title: Sneak Attacks
entries.summary: Sneak Attack is a special kind of Critical Hit enabled by explicit circumstances.

sections.title: Sneak Attack Reference
paragraphs: Sneak Attack only occurs under specific circumstances, such as a Martial Art or Technique explicitly enabling it or the target being completely off guard.

entries.title: Minions
entries.summary: Minion is a tag for generic fodder enemies and martial artists.

sections.title: Minion Reference
paragraphs: Minions are NPCs whose Realm and HP/Stance are lower than the player characters, usually fragile enough to be removed in a single blow, such as an NPC with only 5 HP and 5 Stance.

entries.title: Companion Derivation
entries.summary: Puppets, phantoms, animals, and similar companions derive from the user's Stats unless their creating ability says otherwise.

sections.title: Companion Reference
paragraphs: Companions usually use half the user's Stats plus any listed modifications from the ability that creates or commands them. Richer companion automation is deferred.

entries.title: GM Approval Gates
entries.summary: Karmic Constraints, Ascendant Arts, and Dao Immolating Techniques require explicit GM approval.

sections.title: Approval Requirements
bullets: Karmic Constraints require explicit GM approval in addition to their Insight cost.
Ascendant Arts require explicit GM approval in addition to their Insight cost.
Dao Immolating Techniques may be prepared ahead of time or invented at use time; preparation is not required, but it can make GM approval easier.
Dao Immolating Techniques require explicit GM approval at the time of use and are more narrative than mechanical.

entries.summary: Bing Ti's Legendary rank uses Jing wherever the reviewed draft says Ji.

sections.title: Correction
bullets: In Bing Ti's Legendary rank, read Ji as Jing.
This records the GM clarification only; the Martial Art catalog entry remains deferred to the Martial Arts seed slice.

entries.title: Duel
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Action for locking melee attackers into a close-combat exchange.
entries.search_text: basic action duel melee close combat ranged hard minion disengage

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Duel: After performing a Melee Attack a Character can choose to initiate a Duel against their Target. Doing so will lock both Characters into a heated exchange of close-combat blows in which neither can easily escape nor can outsiders easily interfere in. Ranged Attacks are automatically made HARD while in a Duel, and outside Ranged Attacks shot at either of the characters that are engaged in a Duel has a chance of hitting the wrong Target. A character may choose to Disengage from a Duel with an Action and Move one space away from their former opponent. Minions can not engage Duels themselves but can be engaged in Duels.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Duel: After performing a Melee Attack a Character can choose to initiate a Duel against their Target. Doing so will lock both Characters into a heated exchange of close-combat blows in which neither can easily escape nor can outsiders easily interfere in. Ranged Attacks are automatically made HARD while in a Duel, and outside Ranged Attacks shot at either of the characters that are engaged in a Duel has a chance of hitting the wrong Target. A character may choose to Disengage from a Duel with an Action and Move one space away from their former opponent. Minions can not engage Duels themselves but can be engaged in Duels.

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Wide Dispatch: You may choose to make an Area of Effect Attack against all Minion Targets within 3 Spaces of you. However this Attack can only deal Basic (If Unarmed) or Weapon Effort Damage (If Armed).

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Wide Dispatch: You may choose to make an Area of Effect Attack against all Minion Targets within 3 Spaces of you. However this Attack can only deal Basic (If Unarmed) or Weapon Effort Damage (If Armed).

entries.title: Flowing Dao
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Action for spending Dao to gain an Action or recover HP/Stance.
entries.search_text: basic action flowing dao action recover hp stance

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Flowing Dao: Once per Turn, you may spend a point of Dao to either gain +1 additional Action or Regain 5 HP/Stance.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Flowing Dao: Once per Turn, you may spend a point of Dao to either gain +1 additional Action or Regain 5 HP/Stance.

entries.title: Throat Jab
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Action for forcing a Constitution check against vocalizing or incantations.
entries.search_text: basic action throat jab constitution vocalize incantation

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Throat Jab: Spend an Action, Target must make a successful CON check or be unable to vocalize or use incantations for 1 Round.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Throat Jab: Spend an Action, Target must make a successful CON check or be unable to vocalize or use incantations for 1 Round.

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Knuckle Strike: Spend an Action, Target must make a successful DEX check or be unable to use Mudras for 1 Round.

entries.title: Defend
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Stance that trades Attack for Defense and damage reduction.
entries.search_text: basic action defend stance defense attack damage reduction

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Defend (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active your Defense is +5 and your Attacks are -5. If Hit you take 5 less Damage.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Defend (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active your Defense is +5 and your Attacks are -5. If Hit you take 5 less Damage.

entries.title: All-Out Offense
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Stance that trades Defense for Attack and additional damage.
entries.search_text: basic action all-out offense stance attack defense damage

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: All-Out Offense (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active your Attack is +5 and your Defense is -5. Deal an additional +5 Damage.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: All-Out Offense (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active your Attack is +5 and your Defense is -5. Deal an additional +5 Damage.

entries.title: Parry
entries.summary: Reference-only Xianxia Basic Stance for a stationary defensive posture and counter-attack reminder.
entries.search_text: basic action parry stance defense attack counter-attack weapon effort

xianxia_basic_action.source_text: Parry (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active add +3 to Defense and Attack, you can not Move while this Stance is Active. If your opponent Attacks and misses you may make an immediate Counter-Attack, dealing an additional Weapon Effort in Damage if successful.

sections.title: Source Text
paragraphs: Parry (Stance): Spend an Action, While Active add +3 to Defense and Attack, you can not Move while this Stance is Active. If your opponent Attacks and misses you may make an immediate Counter-Attack, dealing an additional Weapon Effort in Damage if successful.
